prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for pyridine (C₅H₅N, 11 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fit RI-J on by default in PySCF), gas phase, charge 0 multiplicity 1 (cation: charge +1 mult 2; anion: charge −1 mult 2). Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz, used as-is (no re-optimization). Charges from Mulliken population analysis (PySCF) on N, N+1, and N−1 electron states. All three SCF calculations converged. integrity.trustworthy = true. No warnings reported; sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (charge conservation satisfied).

Headline result (most electrophilic atom): N (atom 1), f⁺ = 0.48422572.

Full per-atom Fukui indices:
  Atom 1  N:  f+ = 0.48422572,  f− = 0.06412267,  f0 = 0.27417420,  Δf = +0.42010305
  Atom 2  C:  f+ = 0.32447227,  f− = 0.00618771,  f0 = 0.16532999,  Δf = +0.31828455
  Atom 3  C:  f+ = 0.14032092,  f− = 0.11823139,  f0 = 0.12927615,  Δf = +0.02208954
  Atom 4  C:  f+ = 0.14039586,  f− = 0.11826260,  f0 = 0.12932923,  Δf = +0.02213327  ← most nucleophilic (highest f−)
  Atom 5  C:  f+ = 0.29962626,  f− = 0.11206595,  f0 = 0.20584611,  Δf = +0.18756031
  Atom 6  C:  f+ = 0.29947706,  f− = 0.11200985,  f0 = 0.20574345,  Δf = +0.18746722
  Atom 7  H:  f+ = −0.14151320, f− = 0.08284851,  f0 = −0.02933234, Δf = −0.22436171
  Atom 8  H:  f+ = −0.13368410, f− = 0.09817949,  f0 = −0.01775231, Δf = −0.23186359
  Atom 9  H:  f+ = −0.13365905, f− = 0.09818226,  f0 = −0.01773839, Δf = −0.23184131
  Atom 10 H:  f+ = −0.13981630, f− = 0.09496009,  f0 = −0.02242810, Δf = −0.23477639
  Atom 11 H:  f+ = −0.13984545, f− = 0.09494948,  f0 = −0.02244799, Δf = −0.23479493

Key findings: The nitrogen atom (atom 1) is the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.48423, dual descriptor Δf = +0.42010), consistent with its lone-pair donor / Lewis-base character. Carbon-4 (atom 4, para to N) is the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.11826), consistent with pyridine's π-deficient ring reactivity. All hydrogen atoms show negative dual descriptor values.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz
